title: Crunchy Citrusy Yucatecan Long-bean Cochinita Pibil
description: A weeknight Mexican (Yucatecan) dish featuring grilled long-bean with a citrusy twist. This dish is inspired by the traditional Cochinita Pibil recipe but adapted with a healthier approach to make it macrobiotic-friendly.
instructions: Cut the long beans into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix together cumin, coriander, lime juice, orange juice, honey, salt, and balsamic vinegar to make the marinade.
Add the long beans to the marinade and toss to coat.
Let the long beans marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Grill the long beans for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they develop a nice char.
Remove the long beans from the grill and serve hot.
Optional: Serve with a side of Mexican street corn or a simple green salad.
Note: If using a grill pan, cook the long beans for 4-5 minutes per side.
Before serving, drizzle with additional lime juice and sprinkle with chopped cilantro if desired.
